fields.description:
## Why
Audit Maintainability finding #3 (MEDIUM). The codebase reads ~80 distinct `REBAR_*` env vars across ~133 `os.environ`/`getenv` sites; only ~42 sit in the three intended config modules ‚Äî the rest are scattered (worst in the reconciler), with no single artifact enumerating what can be set. A new env var can ship undocumented. The repo already solved this shape (the derived prompt-index `reviewers/index.json` + its regenerate-and-diff CI drift gate).

## What / files
- `scripts/gen_env_registry.py`: an AST-based scan of `src/rebar/**/*.py` collecting env-var reads. It emits a deterministically-sorted registry to `docs/env-vars.md` with columns: var name, reading module(s) (relative path), alias/deprecation status. Sources of truth, combined:
  1. **Literal reads** ‚Äî `os.environ["X"]`, `os.environ.get("X", ‚Ä¶)`, `os.getenv("X", ‚Ä¶)` where the key is a string literal (covers the vast majority).
  2. **Dynamically-constructed reads** ‚Äî the reconciler pattern `os.environ.get(f"REBAR_{name}", ‚Ä¶)` (in outbound_differ.py, inbound_translate.py, apply_handlers.py, apply_inbound.py, rebar_id_audit.py, outbound_fields.py) CANNOT be statically resolved to concrete names. The generator detects these `f"REBAR_{‚Ä¶}"` call sites and lists them in a dedicated "dynamically-constructed (resolved at runtime ‚Äî see source)" section of the doc, citing file:line, so they are surfaced (not silently missed) even though the exact suffix isn't known statically.
  3. **Alias/deprecation status** ‚Äî read from `src/rebar/_deprecations.py` `REGISTRY` (the `via="env"` entries ‚Äî e.g. `REBAR_NO_SYNC`‚Üí`REBAR_SYNC_PULL`, `COMPACT_THRESHOLD`‚Üí`REBAR_COMPACT_THRESHOLD`), which is the authoritative live-alias source. Do NOT hardcode `REBAR_PUSH` / `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` ‚Äî those were fully REMOVED pre-1.0 (DE7) and are not read anywhere; treating them as active aliases would produce phantom rows.
  A `--check` mode regenerates in-memory and exits nonzero on any diff vs the committed `docs/env-vars.md`.
- `docs/env-vars.md`: the committed generated artifact, with a header note "generated by scripts/gen_env_registry.py ‚Äî do not edit by hand".
- `.github/workflows/test.yml`: an "Env-var registry drift gate" step modeled on the existing "Prompt-index drift gate" (~L224-240) ‚Äî run `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py --check`, failing the build on drift.
- A pointer to `docs/env-vars.md` added to the **`docs/README.md`** index (Operator section).

## Scope
In: the generator, the committed registry doc, the CI drift-gate step, and the docs/README.md pointer. Out: consolidating the scattered `os.environ` reads into config modules (separate refactor); adding/removing any env var; annotating undocumented vars with source doc-comments (out of scope ‚Äî the registry itself is the documentation).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/env-vars.md` is committed; regenerating it (`python scripts/gen_env_registry.py`) produces NO diff (`git diff --exit-code -- docs/env-vars.md` is clean)
- [ ] Every literal `REBAR_*` (and other literal) env read under `src/rebar` appears in the registry with its reading module(s); the dynamic `f"REBAR_{name}"` reconciler sites appear in the dedicated dynamic section with file:line
- [ ] Alias/deprecation status is populated from `src/rebar/_deprecations.py` REGISTRY (env channel); no phantom rows for removed vars (REBAR_PUSH / REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE do not appear as active aliases)
- [ ] A unit test (`tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py`) asserts the NEGATIVE case explicitly: given a temp source file containing a NEW `os.environ["REBAR_FAKE_NEW"]` read not in the committed registry, `gen_env_registry.py --check` exits nonzero (drift detected); and exits 0 against the committed clean tree
- [ ] The drift-gate step exists in test.yml modeled on the prompt-index gate
- [ ] docs/README.md links docs/env-vars.md; `make lint` green
